site.title: Computer Vision arXiv Digest
site.description: Daily ranked top 5 cs.CV papers with expandable full listings.

top5.title: DINOv3 Beats Specialized Detectors: A Simple Foundation Model Baseline for Image Forensics
top5.summary: This paper makes a sharp practical claim: a simple DINOv3 plus LoRA baseline beats prior specialized forensic localization methods by large margins, including a 17-point average pixel-F1 gain under CAT-Net and much stronger performance in low-data settings. It is notable because it argues that foundation-model representations may already encode strong forensic cues, resetting the baseline for image forensics.

top5.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
top5.summary: CrossMath is a cleanly controlled benchmark that isolates text-only, image-only, and joint reasoning with identical task information, and the headline result is striking: visual input often hurts versus text alone. That makes this one of the more rigorous new pieces of evidence that many current VLMs still reason mostly in text space rather than from vision.

top5.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
top5.summary: Mind's Eye targets visual abstraction and transformation with human-intelligence-test-style tasks, and humans reach 80 percent while top MLLMs stay below 50 percent. The benchmark is broad, cognitively motivated, and useful for pressure-testing whether multimodal systems can do more than pattern-match on standard VQA-style tasks.

top5.title: UniEditBench: A Unified and Cost-Effective Benchmark for Image and Video Editing via Distilled MLLMs
top5.summary: Image and video editing evaluation is still fragmented, and this benchmark unifies both modalities while also introducing distilled evaluator models that better align with human judgment at much lower cost. That combination of unified protocol and scalable judging could make it a practical reference point for editing research.

top5.title: Neural Gabor Splatting: Enhanced Gaussian Splatting with Neural Gabor for High-frequency Surface Reconstruction
top5.summary: This extends Gaussian splatting with per-primitive neural Gabor modeling and a frequency-aware densification strategy to better handle high-frequency textures and surfaces. It stands out because it tackles a real bottleneck in 3DGS representations while preserving the speed and editability that make splatting attractive.

top5.title: HY-World 2.0: A Multi-Modal World Model for Reconstructing, Generating, and Simulating 3D Worlds
top5.summary: HY-World 2.0 is the broadest systems paper in today's batch: it unifies text, image, multi-view, and video inputs into open 3D world reconstruction and generation with a full 3DGS pipeline and interactive renderer. The combination of open weights, end-to-end world building, and near-closed-model performance makes it especially notable for researchers working on world models and 3D scene generation.

top5.title: TokenGS: Decoupling 3D Gaussian Prediction from Pixels with Learnable Tokens
top5.summary: TokenGS rethinks feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting by replacing pixel-bound prediction with learnable Gaussian tokens and direct 3D coordinate regression. That architectural shift improves robustness to pose noise and multi-view inconsistency while reaching state-of-the-art reconstruction on static and dynamic scenes, making it a strong conceptual advance for 3DGS.

top5.title: GlobalSplat: Efficient Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting via Global Scene Tokens
top5.summary: GlobalSplat attacks the same 3DGS bottleneck from a different angle, using compact global scene tokens to avoid redundancy and representation bloat in feed-forward reconstruction. The reported 4MB footprint and sub-78ms inference make it particularly interesting for scalable, deployable novel-view synthesis.

top5.title: Geometrically Consistent Multi-View Scene Generation from Freehand Sketches
top5.summary: This paper opens a genuinely hard new task: generating consistent multi-view scenes from a single freehand sketch, where the input is abstract and geometrically distorted. The dataset construction, geometry-aware adapters, and explicit correspondence supervision make it one of the most novel vision-generation papers in the batch.

top5.title: Chain-of-Glimpse: Search-Guided Progressive Object-Grounded Reasoning for Video Understanding
top5.summary: Chain-of-Glimpse is notable because it pushes video reasoning toward explicit object-grounded evidence search rather than pure object-agnostic inference. Its stepwise grounded traces and cross-benchmark gains make it a strong signal paper for interpretable multimodal reasoning in video.

top5.title: One Token per Highly Selective Frame: Towards Extreme Compression for Long Video Understanding
top5.summary: Attacks a central bottleneck in video VLMs by pushing toward one token per frame with learnable progressive compression and question-conditioned frame selection, enabling materially longer-context video understanding.

top5.title: Seedance 2.0: Advancing Video Generation for World Complexity
top5.summary: A large native multimodal audio-video generation system that jointly handles text, image, audio, and video inputs, with broad reference-and-editing support and strong reported generation quality.

top5.title: SpatialEvo: Self-Evolving Spatial Intelligence via Deterministic Geometric Environments
top5.summary: Uses geometry-derived ground truth as a deterministic supervision signal for improving 3D spatial reasoning, avoiding the drift of self-confirming pseudo-labels and showing broad benchmark gains.

top5.title: Geometric Context Transformer for Streaming 3D Reconstruction
top5.summary: Introduces a feed-forward streaming 3D reconstruction model with geometry-aware attention, compact persistent state, and real-time throughput, making long-horizon reconstruction more practical.

top5.title: POINTS-Seeker: Towards Training a Multimodal Agentic Search Model from Scratch
top5.summary: Trains a multimodal agentic search model from scratch for long-horizon evidence retrieval, directly targeting a capability that is becoming increasingly important for multimodal reasoning systems.
